title: angelica acutiloba subsp. iwatensis
intro: Angelica acutiloba subsp. iwatensis, also known as Japanese angelica, is a subspecies of the Angelica acutiloba plant, which is a perennial herb native to Japan. It has been traditionally used in Japanese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve blood circulation. The health benefits of Japanese angelica include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant and anti-aging properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Japanese angelica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
